ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The metric quantifies the number of households adopting the E-RO strategy across time steps. Across multiple simulation runs, this metric initiates at approximately 20 households, reflecting the baseline E-RO strategy’s designated population in homogeneous conditions. Over the first 10–15 time steps, slight fluctuations emerge, with some runs showing minor increases (e.g., rising to 22–24 households) and others minor declines (e.g., dropping to 15–19 households). These variations suggest localized dynamics influenced by random climatic shocks or initial resource conditions. After approximately 20 time steps, a consistent downward trend becomes evident, with values declining steadily across runs to as low as 0 households in later stages (e.g., 5–10 time steps from the end). This prolonged decrease likely corresponds to cumulative resource depletion or household attrition under persistent climatic instability, as non-learning strategies (E-RO) fail to adjust stocking levels in response to deteriorating pasture health. The final stabilization near zero aligns with long-term resource exhaustion risks inherent in the model’s environmental context, where fixed strategies cannot mitigate systemic ecological degradation.
